member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext
member.summary: Entity Framework DbContext implementation for the Kalshi trading bot system. Provides comprehensive data access layer for managing trading entities including markets, events, series, snapshots, brain instances, and various trading-related data. Implements IKalshiBotContext interface for dependency injection and testing.
member.remarks: This context manages the complete lifecycle of trading data, including: - Market and event data synchronization - Trading snapshots and historical data - Brain instance management and coordination - Order and position tracking - SignalR client management - Logging and audit trails - Weight set and strategy management

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the KalshiBotContext with configuration and optional logging.
member.param: Application configuration containing database connection strings.
Optional logger for context operations. If null, logging is disabled.
member.exception: Thrown when DefaultConnection is not configured.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetSeriesByTicker(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves a series by its ticker symbol.
member.param: The unique ticker identifier for the series.
member.returns: The series data transfer object, or null if not found.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.AddOrUpdateSeries(BacklashDTOs.Data.SeriesDTO)
member.summary: Adds or updates a series with its associated tags and settlement sources. Performs idempotent operations with case-insensitive deduplication.
member.param: The series data transfer object containing series information and related data.
member.exception: Thrown when the operation fails, with details about the failure.
member.remarks: This method handles: - Creating new series or updating existing ones - Managing series tags with case-insensitive deduplication - Managing settlement sources with upsert operations - Transaction management with rollback on failure - Retry logic for transient database errors

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetEventByTicker(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves an event by its ticker symbol, including associated series information.
member.param: The unique ticker identifier for the event.
member.returns: The event data transfer object with series information, or null if not found.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetMarketByTicker(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves a market by its ticker symbol, including associated event and series information.
member.param: The unique ticker identifier for the market.
member.returns: The market data transfer object with event and series information, or null if not found.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetMarketsWithSnapshots
member.summary: Retrieves a distinct set of market tickers that have associated snapshots.
member.returns: HashSet of market ticker strings that have at least one snapshot.
member.remarks: This method is useful for identifying markets that have historical trading data available for analysis or backtesting purposes.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetInactiveMarketsWithNoSnapshots
member.summary: Retrieves inactive markets that don't have any snapshots.
member.returns: HashSet of market tickers for inactive markets without snapshot data.
member.remarks: This method identifies markets that are no longer active and have no historical snapshot data, which may be candidates for cleanup or special handling.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetProcessedMarkets
member.summary: Retrieves inactive markets that have been processed into snapshot groups.
member.returns: HashSet of market tickers for inactive markets that have snapshot groups.
member.remarks: This method identifies markets that are no longer active but have been processed into snapshot groups for analysis, indicating they have completed their lifecycle.

member.summary: Retrieves a filtered list of markets based on various criteria.
member.param: Set of market statuses to include. If specified, only markets with these statuses are returned.
Set of market statuses to exclude. If specified, markets with these statuses are filtered out.
Set of specific market tickers to include. If specified, only these markets are returned.
Set of specific market tickers to exclude. If specified, these markets are filtered out.
Filter for markets that have (true) or don't have (false) market watch data.
Minimum interest score threshold for markets with market watch data.
Maximum interest score date for filtering market watch data.
Maximum API last fetch time for filtering recently updated markets.
member.returns: List of market data transfer objects matching the specified criteria.
member.remarks: This method supports complex filtering combinations and is optimized for performance with efficient database queries that combine multiple filter conditions.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.GetMarketStatus(System.String)
member.summary: Checks the existence status of a market and its hierarchical relationships (event and series).
member.returns: A tuple indicating whether the market, its event, and series were found in the database.
member.remarks: This method performs an efficient single query to check the complete hierarchy using RIGHT OUTER JOINs to ensure all relationships are verified in one database call.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.SaveChangesWithRetryAsync
member.summary: Saves changes to the database with retry logic for transient SQL errors.
member.remarks: Implements Polly retry policy to handle transient SQL Server errors such as: deadlocks (1205), lock timeouts (1222), connection issues, and throttling errors. Retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 3s delays).

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.IsTransient(Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlException)
member.summary: Determines if a SQL exception represents a transient error that should be retried.
member.returns: True if the exception is transient and should be retried.
member.remarks: Transient errors include deadlocks, timeouts, connection issues, and Azure SQL throttling. Non-transient errors (like constraint violations) are not retried.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.OnConfiguring(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder)
member.summary: Configures the database context to use SQL Server with the configured connection string.
member.param: The options builder for configuring the context.
member.remarks: This method is called by Entity Framework during context initialization. It sets up the SQL Server provider with the connection string from configuration.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.KalshiBotContext.OnModelCreating(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ModelBuilder)
member.summary: Configures the Entity Framework model with table mappings, keys, relationships, and constraints.
member.param: The model builder for configuring entity mappings.
member.remarks: This method defines: - Table names and schemas - Primary keys and composite keys - Foreign key relationships and cascade behaviors - Database indexes for performance optimization - Column constraints and default values - Concurrency tokens for optimistic locking

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService
member.summary: Provides asynchronous data persistence services for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. This service manages concurrent queues for different data types (order book, trades, fills, lifecycle events) and processes them using configurable background worker tasks with retry logic for transient SQL errors. Supports batch processing, performance metrics collection, input validation, and configurable queue sizes to prevent resource exhaustion. Implements the ISqlDataService interface for dependency injection and proper resource management.
member.remarks: The service uses Polly for retry policies on SQL operations and maintains separate queues to ensure data integrity and prevent blocking between different data types. All operations are asynchronous to support high-throughput real-time data processing without impacting the main application flow. Configuration options include retry counts, delays, queue sizes, worker counts, and batch sizes. Performance metrics are collected for processing rates, queue depths, and success rates. Input validation ensures JSON data integrity before queuing operations.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.ExecuteSnapshotImportJobAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes a SQL Server Agent job to import market snapshot data from external files into the database. Monitors the job execution status and waits for completion, with retry logic for transient failures.
member.param: Token to cancel the operation if needed.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation.
member.exception: Thrown when SQL operations fail after retries.
Thrown when job execution fails or cannot be monitored.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.StoreOrderBookAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.String)
member.summary: Asynchronously stores order book data from WebSocket messages into the database queue for processing. Handles both snapshot (SNP) and delta (DEL) order book updates, converting prices for "no" side orders. Includes input validation and queue size checks.
member.param: The JSON element containing the WebSocket message data.
The type of order book update: "SNP" for snapshots or "DEL" for deltas.
member.returns: A completed task since the operation is queued for background processing.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.StoreTickerAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Asynchronously stores ticker data from WebSocket messages into the database queue for processing. Captures current market prices, bid/ask spreads, volume, and open interest information. Includes input validation and queue size checks.
member.param: The JSON element containing the WebSocket ticker message data.
member.returns: A completed task since the operation is queued for background processing.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.StoreTradeAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Asynchronously stores trade execution data from WebSocket messages into the database queue for processing. Records trade details including prices, volumes, and taker side information. Includes input validation and queue size checks.
member.param: The JSON element containing the WebSocket trade message data.
member.returns: A completed task since the operation is queued for background processing.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.StoreFillAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Asynchronously stores order fill data from WebSocket messages into the database queue for processing. Records fill details including trade IDs, order IDs, prices, and execution information. Includes input validation and queue size checks.
member.param: The JSON element containing the WebSocket fill message data.
member.returns: A completed task since the operation is queued for background processing.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.StoreEventLifecycleAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Asynchronously stores event lifecycle data from WebSocket messages into the database queue for processing. Records event metadata including titles, subtitles, collateral types, and timing information. Includes input validation and queue size checks.
member.param: The JSON element containing the WebSocket event lifecycle message data.
member.returns: A completed task since the operation is queued for background processing.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.StoreMarketLifecycleAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Asynchronously stores market lifecycle data from WebSocket messages into the database queue for processing. Records market state changes including open/close times, determination times, and settlement results. Includes input validation and queue size checks.
member.param: The JSON element containing the WebSocket market lifecycle message data.
member.returns: A completed task since the operation is queued for background processing.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes of the service resources, canceling background worker tasks and cleaning up cancellation tokens. Waits for worker tasks to complete gracefully within a timeout period.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.IsTransient(Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlException)
member.summary: Determines whether a SQL exception represents a transient error that should be retried. Checks against a predefined list of SQL error codes known to be transient in nature.
member.returns: True if the exception is transient and should be retried; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.ProcessQueueAsync(System.Collections.Concurrent.ConcurrentQueue{KalshiBotData.Data.SqlDataService.DatabaseOperation},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Background worker method that continuously processes database operations from a specific queue. Executes stored procedures with retry logic for transient SQL errors and handles specific error conditions. Supports batch processing and collects performance metrics.
member.param: The concurrent queue containing database operations to process.
Descriptive name of the operation type for logging and error handling.
Token to signal when processing should stop.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous processing operation.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement
member.summary: Represents a system announcement or notification in the Kalshi trading bot ecosystem. This entity stores important messages, alerts, or updates that need to be communicated to users or other system components. Announcements can have different types and statuses to categorize their importance and delivery state.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.AnnouncementID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this announcement. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.DeliveryTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this announcement should be delivered or was delivered. This helps in scheduling and tracking the timing of announcements.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the actual message content of the announcement. This contains the text that will be displayed to users or processed by other systems.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.Status
member.summary: Gets or sets the current status of the announcement (e.g., "pending", "delivered", "read"). This tracks the lifecycle state of the announcement.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type or category of the announcement (e.g., "system", "market", "alert"). This helps in filtering and prioritizing different types of announcements.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.CreatedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this announcement was first created in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking the age of announcements.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Announcement.LastModifiedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this announcement was last modified. This helps track updates to announcement content or status.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance
member.summary: Represents a brain instance configuration and operational state in the trading bot system. This entity manages the settings and runtime state for individual brain instances that execute trading strategies, monitor markets, and capture snapshots. Each brain instance can have different configurations for market watching, position tracking, and resource usage.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.BrainInstanceName
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique name identifier for this brain instance. This serves as the primary identifier for the brain in the system.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.BrainLock
member.summary: Gets or sets the brain lock GUID that prevents multiple instances from operating on the same markets simultaneously. When set, this brain has exclusive access to certain market operations.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.WatchPositions
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this brain instance should monitor and track trading positions. When enabled, the brain will maintain position data and execute position-based strategies.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.WatchOrders
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this brain instance should monitor and track trading orders. When enabled, the brain will maintain order data and execute order-based strategies.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.ManagedWatchList
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this brain instance uses a managed watch list. When enabled, the system automatically manages which markets this brain monitors based on interest scores and performance metrics.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.CaptureSnapshots
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this brain instance should capture market snapshots. When enabled, the brain will periodically save comprehensive market state for analysis and backtesting purposes.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.TargetWatches
member.summary: Gets or sets the target number of markets this brain instance should watch. This is used by the managed watch list system to determine how many markets to allocate to this brain instance.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.MinimumInterest
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum interest score threshold for markets that this brain instance will consider watching. Markets below this threshold will be ignored.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.UsageMin
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum resource usage threshold for this brain instance. This helps in load balancing and preventing resource exhaustion.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.UsageMax
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum resource usage threshold for this brain instance. This prevents any single brain from consuming too many system resources.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainInstance.LastSeen
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this brain instance was last seen active. This is used for monitoring brain health and detecting failed instances.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainPersistenceEntity
member.summary: Database entity for storing BrainPersistence data. Uses JSON serialization to store complex nested objects.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainPersistenceEntity.BrainInstanceName
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique name identifier for this brain instance. This serves as the primary key for the brain persistence data.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainPersistenceEntity.PersistenceData
member.summary: Gets or sets the JSON serialized BrainPersistence data. Contains all the configuration, state, and historical metrics.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainPersistenceEntity.LastUpdated
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this data was last saved. Used for tracking data freshness and cleanup operations.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.BrainPersistenceEntity.Version
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the persistence format. Allows for future schema migrations.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle
member.summary: Represents the complete lifecycle state and timing information for a trading event in the Kalshi system. This entity tracks the progression of an event from opening to settlement, including all critical timestamps and state changes. It serves as the authoritative source for event status and timing used throughout the trading bot for market state management and strategy execution.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.market_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker identifier for this event lifecycle. This uniquely identifies the market within the Kalshi trading system.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.open_ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when this event was opened for trading. This marks the beginning of the trading period for this event.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.close_ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when this event was closed for trading. After this time, no new orders can be placed for this event.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.determination_ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when the outcome of this event was determined. This occurs after the event closes and the result becomes known.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.settled_ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when this event was fully settled. This is when all payouts are processed and the event is completely resolved.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.result
member.summary: Gets or sets the final result or outcome of this event. This determines which side of the market (Yes/No) was correct.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.is_deactivated
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this event has been deactivated or removed from active trading. Deactivated events are no longer available for trading but may still be visible for historical purposes.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.LoggedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this lifecycle data was logged in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking when the data was first captured.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.ProcessedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this lifecycle data was processed by the system. This indicates when the data was validated and made available for use.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.EventLifecycle.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the full market details and related data.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule
member.summary: Represents the operational schedule and maintenance configuration for the Kalshi exchange. This entity manages the overall timing and availability of the trading platform, including regular operating hours and scheduled maintenance periods. It serves as the master schedule that coordinates all trading activities and system maintenance across the platform.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule.ExchangeScheduleID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this exchange schedule. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule.LastUpdated
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this schedule was last updated from the exchange. This indicates the freshness of the schedule data.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule.CreatedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this schedule record was created in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking schedule history.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule.LastModifiedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this schedule was last modified. This helps track changes to the schedule configuration.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule.MaintenanceWindows
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of maintenance windows associated with this exchange schedule. These define periods when the exchange is unavailable for trading due to maintenance.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.ExchangeSchedule.StandardHours
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of standard operating hours for this exchange schedule. These define the regular trading hours and sessions for the exchange.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill
member.summary: Represents a trade execution (fill) in the Kalshi trading system. This entity captures the details of individual trade executions, including pricing, quantity, and market information. Fills are generated when orders are matched and executed in the market, providing a complete audit trail of all trading activity.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.trade_id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for the trade that this fill is part of. This links the fill to the broader trade execution.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.order_id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for the order that generated this fill. This links the fill back to the original order that was placed.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.market_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker identifier where this fill occurred. This identifies the specific market contract that was traded.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.is_taker
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this fill was executed as a taker order. Taker orders remove liquidity from the market, while maker orders add liquidity.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.side
member.summary: Gets or sets the side of the market this fill was executed on. This indicates whether the fill was for the "yes" or "no" side of the contract.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.yes_price
member.summary: Gets or sets the price at which the "yes" side of the contract was traded. This is expressed in the standard Kalshi pricing format (cents).

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.no_price
member.summary: Gets or sets the price at which the "no" side of the contract was traded. This is expressed in the standard Kalshi pricing format (cents).

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.count
member.summary: Gets or sets the quantity of contracts that were filled in this execution. This represents the number of shares or contracts traded.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.action
member.summary: Gets or sets the action type for this fill (e.g., "buy", "sell"). This indicates the direction of the trade from the perspective of the trader.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when this fill was executed. This provides precise timing information for the trade execution.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.LoggedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this fill data was logged in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking when the fill was first captured.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.ProcessedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this fill data was processed by the system. This indicates when the fill was validated and made available for analysis.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Fill.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the full market details and related trading data.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry
member.summary: Represents a structured log entry in the Kalshi trading bot system. This entity captures comprehensive logging information including severity levels, contextual data, and system state for debugging and monitoring purposes. Log entries are used throughout the system for troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and maintaining an audit trail of system activities.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this log entry. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Timestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this log entry was created. This provides precise timing information for log analysis and correlation.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Level
member.summary: Gets or sets the severity level of this log entry. Common levels include Debug, Information, Warning, Error, and Critical.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the main log message describing the event or state being logged. This contains the primary information about what occurred.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Exception
member.summary: Gets or sets any exception details associated with this log entry. This is populated when logging errors or exceptions for debugging purposes.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Environment
member.summary: Gets or sets the environment context where this log entry was generated. This helps distinguish between development, staging, and production environments.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.BrainInstance
member.summary: Gets or sets the brain instance identifier that generated this log entry. This allows filtering logs by specific brain instances for targeted analysis.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.SessionIdentifier
member.summary: Gets or sets the session identifier for grouping related log entries. This helps track log entries that belong to the same execution session.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.LogEntry.Source
member.summary: Gets or sets the source component or module that generated this log entry. This provides context about which part of the system produced the log.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow
member.summary: Represents a scheduled maintenance window for the Kalshi exchange. This entity defines periods when the exchange is unavailable for trading due to planned maintenance, upgrades, or system updates. Maintenance windows are critical for coordinating system downtime and ensuring trading bots can react appropriately to planned service interruptions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.MaintenanceWindowID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this maintenance window. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.ExchangeScheduleID
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreign key reference to the parent exchange schedule. This links the maintenance window to its associated schedule configuration.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.StartDateTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the start date and time of this maintenance window. Trading should be suspended before this time to avoid disruptions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.EndDateTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the end date and time of this maintenance window. Trading can resume after this time when the exchange becomes available again.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.CreatedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this maintenance window was created in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking maintenance scheduling history.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.LastModifiedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this maintenance window was last modified. This helps track changes to maintenance schedules and timing.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MaintenanceWindow.ExchangeSchedule
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated ExchangeSchedule entity. This provides access to the parent schedule and related maintenance windows.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Market
member.summary: Represents a tradable market with comprehensive pricing, volume, and status information. This entity serves as the core data model for all market-related operations in the trading bot, containing real-time and historical market data, order book information, and market metadata. Maps to the Markets database table and is central to the trading system's data model.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.Ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker identifier for this position. This uniquely identifies the market contract this position relates to.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.TotalTraded
member.summary: Gets or sets the total number of contracts traded in this market. This represents the cumulative trading volume for position tracking.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.Position
member.summary: Gets or sets the current net position in this market. Positive values indicate a long position, negative values indicate a short position.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.MarketExposure
member.summary: Gets or sets the current market exposure in cents. This represents the dollar value of the current position at current market prices.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.RealizedPnl
member.summary: Gets or sets the realized profit and loss for this position in cents. This tracks the actual gains or losses that have been locked in through trading.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.RestingOrdersCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of resting orders currently active in this market. This includes limit orders that have not yet been filled.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.FeesPaid
member.summary: Gets or sets the total fees paid for trading in this market in cents. This tracks the cumulative trading costs associated with this position.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.LastUpdatedUTC
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this position data was last updated, in UTC. This indicates the freshness of the position information.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketPosition.LastModified
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this position record was last modified. This is used for tracking changes to position data over time.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketType
member.summary: Represents a category or type classification for markets in the Kalshi trading system. This entity provides a way to group and categorize different types of trading markets based on their characteristics, such as binary outcome markets, range markets, or other specialized market types. Market types help in filtering, analysis, and applying different trading strategies based on market characteristics.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketType.MarketTypeID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this market type. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketType.MarketTypeDescription
member.summary: Gets or sets the descriptive name or label for this market type. This provides a human-readable description of the market category (e.g., "Binary Outcome", "Numeric Range", "Date-based").

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch
member.summary: Represents the watch status and monitoring configuration for a specific market. This entity tracks which markets are being actively monitored by the trading bot, including interest scores, brain instance assignments, and activity metrics. MarketWatch entities are crucial for managing the bot's market coverage and optimizing resource allocation across different trading opportunities.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.market_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker identifier for this watch entry. This uniquely identifies the market being monitored.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.BrainLock
member.summary: Gets or sets the brain instance GUID that has locked this market for exclusive monitoring. When set, only the specified brain instance can trade or monitor this market.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.InterestScore
member.summary: Gets or sets the calculated interest score for this market. This score represents the market's attractiveness for trading based on volume, volatility, and other market characteristics.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.InterestScoreDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when the interest score was last calculated. This indicates the freshness of the interest score data.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.LastWatched
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this market was last actively watched or monitored. This helps track market engagement and can be used for cleanup of stale watches.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.AverageWebsocketEventsPerMinute
member.summary: Gets or sets the average number of WebSocket events received per minute for this market. This metric indicates market activity level and can be used for resource allocation decisions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.MarketWatch.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the full market details and trading data.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Order
member.summary: Represents a trading order in the Kalshi system with comprehensive execution tracking. This entity captures all aspects of an order's lifecycle including pricing, status, execution details, and performance metrics. Orders are the fundamental unit of trading activity and this entity provides complete visibility into order management and execution.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.OrderId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this order. This is the primary key used to track the order throughout its lifecycle.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.Ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker symbol for this order. This identifies the specific market contract being traded.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.UserId
member.summary: Gets or sets the user identifier who placed this order. This links the order to the trading account that initiated it.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.Action
member.summary: Gets or sets the action type for this order (e.g., "buy", "sell"). This indicates the direction of the trade from the user's perspective.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.Side
member.summary: Gets or sets the market side for this order ("yes" or "no"). This specifies which side of the binary contract the order is for.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the order type (e.g., "limit", "market"). This defines how the order should be executed in the market.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.Status
member.summary: Gets or sets the current status of the order (e.g., "pending", "filled", "cancelled"). This tracks the order's progress through its lifecycle.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.YesPrice
member.summary: Gets or sets the price for the "yes" side of the contract in cents. This is the limit price at which the user is willing to buy/sell the yes side.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.NoPrice
member.summary: Gets or sets the price for the "no" side of the contract in cents. This is the limit price at which the user is willing to buy/sell the no side.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.CreatedTimeUTC
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this order was created, in UTC. This marks the beginning of the order's lifecycle.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.LastUpdateTimeUTC
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this order was last updated, in UTC. This tracks any modifications or status changes to the order.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.ExpirationTimeUTC
member.summary: Gets or sets the expiration timestamp for this order, in UTC. After this time, the order will be automatically cancelled if not filled.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.ClientOrderId
member.summary: Gets or sets the client-provided order identifier. This allows clients to track orders using their own reference numbers.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.PlaceCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order has been placed or re-placed. This tracks order modification history.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.DecreaseCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order's quantity has been decreased. This tracks partial order modifications.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.AmendCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order has been amended. This includes price changes and other order modifications.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.AmendTakerFillCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order was amended and then immediately filled as a taker. This tracks aggressive order modifications that resulted in immediate execution.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.MakerFillCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of contracts filled as a maker (added liquidity). This tracks the portion of the order that was filled by resting on the book.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.TakerFillCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of contracts filled as a taker (took liquidity). This tracks the portion of the order that was filled by hitting existing orders.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.RemainingCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the remaining quantity of contracts still unfilled. This shows how much of the original order quantity is still active.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.QueuePosition
member.summary: Gets or sets the current position of this order in the market queue. This indicates how many orders are ahead of this one at the same price level.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.MakerFillCost
member.summary: Gets or sets the total cost of maker fills in cents. This represents the value of contracts filled when this order was resting on the book.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.TakerFillCost
member.summary: Gets or sets the total cost of taker fills in cents. This represents the value of contracts filled when this order hit existing resting orders.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.MakerFees
member.summary: Gets or sets the total fees paid for maker fills in cents. This tracks the trading costs associated with providing liquidity.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.TakerFees
member.summary: Gets or sets the total fees paid for taker fills in cents. This tracks the trading costs associated with taking liquidity.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.FccCancelCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order was cancelled due to FCC regulations. This tracks regulatory cancellations for pattern day trading rules.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.CloseCancelCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order was cancelled due to market closure. This tracks automatic cancellations when markets close.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.TakerSelfTradeCancelCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of times this order was cancelled due to taker self-trading prevention. This tracks cancellations that prevent a user from trading against their own orders.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.LastModified
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this order record was last modified. This is used for auditing and tracking order data changes.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Order.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the market details and related trading information.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry
member.summary: Represents a log entry from the overseer system for monitoring and debugging. This entity stores detailed logging information from the overseer component, including error details, environment context, and session tracking. Maps to the t_OverseerLogs database table.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this log entry. This serves as the primary key in the t_OverseerLogs table.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Timestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this log entry was created. Used for chronological ordering and time-based filtering of logs.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Level
member.summary: Gets or sets the logging level (e.g., "Debug", "Information", "Warning", "Error"). This categorizes the severity and importance of the log entry.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the actual log message content. This contains the descriptive text of what occurred or what was logged.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Exception
member.summary: Gets or sets exception details if this log entry is related to an error. Contains stack trace and exception information for debugging purposes.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Environment
member.summary: Gets or sets the environment context where this log entry was generated. Helps identify which deployment environment (dev, staging, prod) the log came from.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.BrainInstance
member.summary: Gets or sets the brain instance name that generated this log entry. Used to correlate logs with specific trading bot instances.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.SessionIdentifier
member.summary: Gets or sets the session identifier for tracking related log entries. Groups logs from the same operational session for easier analysis.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.OverseerLogEntry.Source
member.summary: Gets or sets the source component or module that generated this log entry. Helps identify which part of the system produced the log message.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesSettlementSource
member.summary: Represents a settlement source reference for a trading series. This entity links a series to external data sources that will be used to determine the final outcome and settlement value of the series contracts. Settlement sources provide the authoritative data that resolves the market and determines payouts to winning positions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesSettlementSource.series_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the series ticker identifier that this settlement source belongs to. This links the settlement source to its parent series.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesSettlementSource.name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name or title of this settlement source. This provides a human-readable description of the data source.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesSettlementSource.url
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL or reference to the external data source. This points to the authoritative source that will determine the settlement value.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesSettlementSource.Series
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Series entity. This provides access to the parent series and related settlement information.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesTag
member.summary: Represents a tag or category label associated with a trading series. This entity provides a flexible tagging system for categorizing and organizing trading series based on various characteristics such as market type, theme, or other classification criteria. Tags enable efficient filtering and discovery of series with similar attributes.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesTag.series_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the series ticker identifier that this tag is associated with. This links the tag to its parent series.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesTag.tag
member.summary: Gets or sets the tag value or label for this series. This contains the actual tag text (e.g., "politics", "sports", "finance").

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SeriesTag.Series
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Series entity. This provides access to the parent series and related tag information.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient
member.summary: Represents a client connected to the system via SignalR for real-time communication. This entity tracks client connections, authentication, and connection state for managing real-time data distribution to dashboards, overseer systems, and other connected clients. It enables secure, authenticated real-time communication between the trading bot system and its clients.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.ClientId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this client. This serves as the primary key for client identification.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.ClientName
member.summary: Gets or sets the human-readable name for this client. This provides a friendly identifier for the client (e.g., "Dashboard-001").

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.IPAddress
member.summary: Gets or sets the IP address of the connected client. This is used for security monitoring and connection tracking.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.AuthToken
member.summary: Gets or sets the authentication token for this client. This token is used to authenticate and authorize the client's connection.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.ClientType
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of client connecting to the system. Common types include 'overseer', 'dashboard', 'mobile', etc.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.IsActive
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this client connection is currently active. This flag indicates if the client is connected and receiving updates.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.LastSeen
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this client was last seen active. This is used to detect stale connections and manage connection cleanup.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.RegisteredAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this client was first registered in the system. This marks the initial connection time for the client.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SignalRClient.ConnectionId
member.summary: Gets or sets the SignalR connection identifier for this client. This is the unique connection ID assigned by the SignalR hub.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot
member.summary: Represents a comprehensive snapshot of market state and trading activity at a specific point in time. This entity captures detailed market data including pricing, volume, velocity metrics, and order book information for analysis, backtesting, and trading strategy evaluation. Snapshots serve as the primary data structure for understanding market behavior and making informed trading decisions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.MarketTicker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker identifier for this snapshot. This identifies the specific market contract that this snapshot represents.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.SnapshotDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this snapshot was captured. This provides the exact point in time that this market state represents.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.JSONSchemaVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the JSON schema used for this snapshot. This ensures compatibility and proper deserialization of snapshot data.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.ChangeMetricsMature
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the change metrics in this snapshot are considered mature. Mature metrics indicate that the snapshot has sufficient historical data for reliable analysis.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.PositionSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the trading position at the time of this snapshot. This represents the net exposure in the market contract.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.VelocityPerMinute_Top_Yes_Bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the velocity (rate of change) of the top yes bid price per minute. This measures how quickly the best yes bid is changing.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.VelocityPerMinute_Top_No_Bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the velocity (rate of change) of the top no bid price per minute. This measures how quickly the best no bid is changing.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.VelocityPerMinute_Bottom_Yes_Bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the velocity (rate of change) of the bottom yes bid price per minute. This measures how quickly the worst yes bid is changing.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.VelocityPerMinute_Bottom_No_Bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the velocity (rate of change) of the bottom no bid price per minute. This measures how quickly the worst no bid is changing.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.OrderVolume_Yes_Bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the order volume for yes bids in the recent period. This represents the total number of yes orders placed.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.OrderVolume_No_Bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the order volume for no bids in the recent period. This represents the total number of no orders placed.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.TradeVolume_Yes
member.summary: Gets or sets the trade volume for yes positions in the recent period. This represents the total number of yes contracts traded.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.TradeVolume_No
member.summary: Gets or sets the trade volume for no positions in the recent period. This represents the total number of no contracts traded.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.AverageTradeSize_Yes
member.summary: Gets or sets the average trade size for yes positions. This represents the typical number of contracts per yes trade.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.AverageTradeSize_No
member.summary: Gets or sets the average trade size for no positions. This represents the typical number of contracts per no trade.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.MarketTypeID
member.summary: Gets or sets the market type identifier for this snapshot. This categorizes the market (e.g., binary outcome, range, etc.).

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.IsValidated
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this snapshot has been validated for data integrity. Validated snapshots are considered reliable for analysis and trading decisions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.RawJSON
member.summary: Gets or sets the raw JSON data containing the complete snapshot information. This field uses a custom JSON converter to preserve the original data format.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.BrainInstance

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the market details and related trading information.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Snapshot.MarketType

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.SnapshotSchema
member.summary: Represents the schema definition and versioning for market snapshots. This entity defines the structure and format of snapshot data, ensuring consistency and compatibility across different versions of the system. Schema definitions are crucial for data validation, migration, and maintaining backward compatibility as the snapshot format evolves.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SnapshotSchema.SchemaVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the version number of this snapshot schema. This serves as the primary key and version identifier for the schema.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.SnapshotSchema.SchemaDefinition
member.summary: Gets or sets the JSON schema definition that describes the structure of snapshot data. This contains the complete schema specification for validating and parsing snapshots.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours
member.summary: Represents the standard operating hours configuration for the Kalshi exchange. This entity defines the regular trading schedule and time periods when the exchange is open for business. Standard hours are used to coordinate trading activities, schedule maintenance, and ensure proper market operation timing across the platform.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.StandardHoursID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this standard hours configuration. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.ExchangeScheduleID
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreign key reference to the parent exchange schedule. This links the standard hours to their associated schedule configuration.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.StartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the start time for these standard operating hours. This marks when the exchange begins regular trading operations.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.EndTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the end time for these standard operating hours. This marks when the exchange concludes regular trading operations.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.CreatedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this standard hours configuration was created. This is used for auditing and tracking schedule history.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.LastModifiedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this standard hours configuration was last modified. This helps track changes to the operating schedule.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.ExchangeSchedule
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated ExchangeSchedule entity. This provides access to the parent schedule and related configuration.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHours.Sessions
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of sessions that make up these standard hours. Sessions define specific time periods within the overall operating hours.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession
member.summary: Represents a specific session within the standard operating hours of the Kalshi exchange. This entity defines individual time periods on specific days when trading is active. Sessions allow for flexible scheduling where different days may have different trading windows, and multiple sessions can exist within a single day's operating hours.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.SessionID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this session. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.StandardHoursID
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreign key reference to the parent standard hours configuration. This links the session to its associated operating hours schedule.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.DayOfWeek
member.summary: Gets or sets the day of the week for this session. This specifies which day (e.g., "Monday", "Tuesday") this session applies to.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.StartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the start time for this trading session. This marks when trading begins for this specific session on the specified day.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.EndTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the end time for this trading session. This marks when trading concludes for this specific session on the specified day.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.CreatedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this session was created. This is used for auditing and tracking session configuration history.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.LastModifiedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this session was last modified. This helps track changes to the session schedule.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StandardHoursSession.StandardHours
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated StandardHours entity. This provides access to the parent operating hours configuration.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.StratData
member.summary: Represents strategy configuration and metadata for trading strategies in the Kalshi bot system. This entity stores the definition and parameters of trading strategies, allowing for flexible strategy management and execution. Strategies can be stored as JSON configurations and instantiated dynamically based on market conditions and trading requirements.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StratData.StratID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this strategy. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StratData.StratName
member.summary: Gets or sets the human-readable name of this strategy. This provides a friendly identifier for the strategy (e.g., "BollingerBreakout", "RSIMomentum").

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StratData.StratType
member.summary: Gets or sets the type identifier for this strategy. This categorizes the strategy (e.g., 1=Momentum, 2=MeanReversion, 3=Breakout).

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.StratData.RawJSON
member.summary: Gets or sets the raw JSON configuration data for this strategy. This contains the complete strategy definition including parameters, rules, and settings.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker
member.summary: Represents real-time ticker data for a Kalshi market, capturing price and volume information at a specific point in time. This entity stores the most recent market data including bid/ask prices, trading volume, and open interest. Ticker data is crucial for real-time trading decisions and market analysis, providing the current state of market pricing and liquidity for individual contracts.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.market_id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique market identifier from the Kalshi API. This is the internal market ID used by the exchange.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.market_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker symbol for this ticker data. This identifies the specific market contract (e.g., "KXUSD-25DEC31").

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.price
member.summary: Gets or sets the current market price in cents. This represents the last traded price for the contract.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.yes_bid
member.summary: Gets or sets the best bid price for the "yes" side of the contract in cents. This is the highest price buyers are willing to pay for yes positions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.yes_ask
member.summary: Gets or sets the best ask price for the "yes" side of the contract in cents. This is the lowest price sellers are willing to accept for yes positions.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.volume
member.summary: Gets or sets the trading volume for this market. This represents the number of contracts traded in the recent period.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.open_interest
member.summary: Gets or sets the open interest for this market. This represents the total number of outstanding contracts.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.dollar_volume
member.summary: Gets or sets the dollar volume traded in this market. This represents the monetary value of contracts traded.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.dollar_open_interest
member.summary: Gets or sets the dollar value of open interest in this market. This represents the monetary value of all outstanding contracts.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when this ticker data was captured. This provides precise timing information for the market data.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.LoggedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this ticker data was logged in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking when the data was first captured.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.ProcessedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this ticker data was processed by the system. This indicates when the data was validated and made available for analysis.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Ticker.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the full market details and related trading information.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade
member.summary: Represents a completed trade execution in the Kalshi market. This entity captures the details of individual trades including pricing, quantity, and market information. Trades are the fundamental records of market activity and provide the basis for volume analysis, price discovery, and market trend identification.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.market_id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique market identifier from the Kalshi API. This is the internal market ID used by the exchange.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.market_ticker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker symbol for this trade. This identifies the specific market contract where the trade occurred.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.yes_price
member.summary: Gets or sets the price at which the "yes" side of the contract was traded in cents. This represents the execution price for yes positions in the trade.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.no_price
member.summary: Gets or sets the price at which the "no" side of the contract was traded in cents. This represents the execution price for no positions in the trade.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.count
member.summary: Gets or sets the quantity of contracts traded in this execution. This represents the number of shares or contracts that changed hands.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.taker_side
member.summary: Gets or sets the side of the market that was the taker in this trade. This indicates whether the trade was initiated by a buyer ("yes") or seller ("no").

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.ts
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unix timestamp when this trade was executed. This provides precise timing information for the trade execution.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.LoggedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this trade data was logged in the system. This is used for auditing and tracking when the trade was first captured.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.ProcessedDate
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this trade data was processed by the system. This indicates when the trade was validated and made available for analysis.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.Trade.Market
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated Market entity. This provides access to the full market details and related trading information.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSet
member.summary: Represents a set of trading weights for portfolio optimization and strategy allocation. This entity defines how trading capital and resources should be distributed across different markets and strategies. Weight sets enable sophisticated portfolio management by allowing dynamic allocation based on market conditions, risk parameters, and performance metrics.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSet.WeightSetID
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this weight set. This serves as the primary key in the database.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSet.StrategyName
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the strategy or portfolio that this weight set represents. This provides a human-readable identifier for the weight configuration.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSet.Weights
member.summary: Gets or sets the JSON string containing the weight allocation data. This defines how capital and resources are distributed across different markets or strategies.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSet.LastRun
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this weight set was last executed or applied. This tracks when the weight allocation was most recently used in trading.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSet.WeightSetMarkets
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of market-specific weights within this weight set. This provides detailed allocation information for individual markets.

member.name: T:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSetMarket
member.summary: Represents the market-specific allocation and performance data within a weight set. This entity defines how individual markets are weighted within a portfolio strategy, tracking both the allocation percentage and the realized performance. It enables granular control over portfolio composition and performance attribution at the market level.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSetMarket.WeightSetID
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreign key reference to the parent weight set. This links the market allocation to its associated weight configuration.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSetMarket.MarketTicker
member.summary: Gets or sets the market ticker symbol for this allocation. This identifies the specific market contract included in the weight set.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSetMarket.PnL
member.summary: Gets or sets the profit and loss for this market within the weight set. This tracks the performance contribution of this specific market allocation.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSetMarket.LastRun
member.summary: Gets or sets the timestamp when this market allocation was last executed. This tracks when the weight was most recently applied to this market.

member.name: P:KalshiBotData.Models.WeightSetMarket.WeightSet
member.summary: Gets or sets the navigation property to the associated WeightSet entity. This provides access to the parent weight configuration and related allocations.
